faqs.question: How much does carpenter ant control cost in Riverdale?

faqs.question: How can I tell if I have carpenter ants versus regular ants in my Riverdale home?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are significantly larger than common household ants (up to ½ inch long) and are typically black or dark brown. You may notice piles of wood shavings (frass) near baseboards, window sills, or wooden structures. Unlike termites, carpenter ants don't eat wood—they excavate it to build nests. If you see large winged ants in spring, especially near windows, you likely have an established colony that needs professional treatment.

faqs.question: What attracts carpenter ants to Riverdale homes?
faqs.answer: Carpenter ants are attracted to moisture-damaged or decaying wood, which is common in Riverdale's older homes and properties surrounded by mature trees. They also seek out food sources like sweet substances and proteins. Leaky roofs, poor drainage, wood-to-soil contact, and tree branches touching your home all create entry points and nesting opportunities for carpenter ants.

faqs.question: How quickly can you eliminate a carpenter ant problem in Riverdale?
faqs.answer: Most carpenter ant treatments show significant results within 7-14 days, though complete colony elimination typically takes 2-4 weeks. We often offer same-day or next-day service in Riverdale. The timeline depends on colony size and location—outdoor colonies may take longer to treat than indoor nests. We include follow-up visits to ensure complete eradication and prevent re-infestation.
